seoTitle: How to Change the Reporter in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to update the reporter field on a Jira issue, which is useful for correcting misattributed tickets or reassigning ownership of a report.
intro: The reporter field in Jira identifies who originally raised an issue, and sometimes it needs to be corrected or reassigned. This guide covers how to update that field on an existing issue.

steps.heading: Open the issue
steps.body: Navigate to the specific Jira issue where you need to update the reporter.

steps.heading: Locate the reporter field
steps.body: Find the reporter field, typically shown in the details panel alongside other issue metadata like assignee and priority.

steps.heading: Select a new reporter
steps.body: Click into the field and search for the user you want to set as the new reporter.

steps.heading: Save the change
steps.body: Confirm the update so the issue reflects the new reporter going forward.

steps.heading: Verify permissions if the field is locked
steps.body: If you can't edit the field, check with your Jira admin, since editing the reporter typically requires specific project permissions.

faq.question: Why would I need to change the reporter on an issue?
faq.answer: This is often done to correct a misattributed ticket, such as when someone files an issue on behalf of a teammate or a support request gets logged under the wrong name.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to change the reporter?
faq.answer: Yes, editing the reporter field typically requires edit permissions on the issue, which may be restricted depending on your project's permission scheme.

faq.question: Does changing the reporter affect the issue's history?
faq.answer: The change itself is usually recorded in the issue's activity log, so there's a record of who the original and updated reporter were.

faq.question: Can I bulk change the reporter across multiple issues?
faq.answer: Jira supports bulk editing for some fields, so check whether your version allows bulk changes to the reporter field if you need to update several issues at once.
